bibliographic_data.title: Machine Learning at the Network Edge: A Survey
bibliographic_data.abstract: Due to the explosive growth of wireless communication technology, the number of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has increased dramatically in recent years. This has led to the generation of large quantities of data in real-time, which is an appealing target for AI systems. However, deploying machine learning models on such end-devices is nearly impossible. To address this issue, efforts have been made to place additional computing devices at the edge of the network, i.e close to the IoT devices where the data is generated. Deploying machine learning systems on such edge computing devices alleviates the above issues by allowing computations to be performed close to the data sources. This survey describes major research efforts where machine learning systems have been deployed at the edge of computer networks, focusing on the operational aspects including compression techniques, tools, frameworks, and hardware used in successful applications of intelligent edge systems.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper surveys major research efforts where machine learning systems have been deployed at the edge of computer networks, focusing on operational aspects such as compression techniques and hardware used in successful applications.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors provide an overview of edge computing architecture and discuss various use cases and benefits of deploying machine learning models at the network edge.
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper discusses the challenges of deploying machine learning models on resource-constrained IoT devices, leading to the need for edge computing. It provides an overview of edge computing architecture and its benefits, including reduced data transfer volume, lower latency, enhanced privacy, and improved system robustness. The authors also discuss various use cases and applications where edge computing has been successfully implemented.
